core_mechanism: formation of a stable self-localized attractor (a dissipative soliton) in a driven-dissipative, non-Hermitian nonlinear medium via simultaneous balance of nonlinearity against spatial spreading and of energy gain against loss, with these localized states emerging through nonlinear bifurcation from linear boundary-confined modes
brief_reason: The abstract characterizes the states as self-localized nonlinear solutions sustained by a dynamic balance between nonlinearity and gain-loss whose stability stems from their attractor nature, arising as families that bifurcate from linear localized modes as nonlinearity increases under boundary-induced confinement.
